title: Two-finger swipe to switch between sibling screens
design_brief: Two-finger horizontal swipe across the app body to switch between sibling tabs (alternative gesture for power users). Reference: iPad two-finger gestures, Notion mobile.
mobile_reasoning: Gesture.Pan().minPointers(2) requires two fingers. Translation derives next tab; on end, snap to nearest with spring.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-091, define named constants for Two-finger swipe to switch between sibling screens: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: For rn-091, isolate cross_platform differences for Two-finger swipe to switch between sibling screens: shadow/elevation, keyboard/safe-area behavior, native picker/sheet/map APIs, and permission prompts stay behind a tiny adapter rather than branching through render code.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: For rn-091, test Two-finger swipe to switch between sibling screens at 320pt width, large Dynamic Type, landscape/tablet split view, and notched safe areas; derive widths from useWindowDimensions or parent layout and preserve 44px touch targets.

gesture_strategy: Pan with minPointers(2).
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-091 (Two-finger swipe to switch between sibling screens): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Keeps continuous gesture math for Two-finger swipe to switch between sibling screens in gesture/Reanimated worklets, commits state once at release, and tests vertical-scroll coexistence plus reduced-motion fallback.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Multi-touch gesture. 2) Power-user shortcut without conflicting with content.

title: Bottom sheet with floating CTA pinned to bottom edge
design_brief: A bottom sheet with scrollable content + a 'Confirm' CTA pinned to the bottom that stays visible regardless of scroll. Reference: Stripe checkout sheet, Apple Pay confirmation.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: CTA without subtle separator from content
mobile_reasoning: Sheet has flex column: ScrollView { flex 1 } then CTA bar { fixed }. Safe-area-padding bottom on CTA.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-092, define named constants for Bottom sheet with floating CTA pinned to bottom edge: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.

animation_strategy: Backdrop opacity derives from sheet translation, keeping dimming and position synchronized.
gesture_strategy: Inherit from rn-002.
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-092 (Bottom sheet with floating CTA pinned to bottom edge): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Uses modal/sheet primitives for Bottom sheet with floating CTA pinned to bottom edge; profile keyboard avoidance, snap changes, and backdrop updates while preserving modal accessibility boundaries.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Safe area respected. 2) Persistent CTA matches Stripe/Apple Pay pattern.

title: Search-as-you-type list with debounced fetch and result fade-in
design_brief: A search results screen: type query → after 200ms debounce, fetch results → results fade-in. Old results crossfade to new. Reference: Spotify search, Apple Mail search.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Hard swap of old → new results
Empty state with no skeleton during fetch
mobile_reasoning: Debounced query via useDeferredValue + setTimeout. Old results dim during fetch; new results fade in.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-093, define named constants for Search-as-you-type list with debounced fetch and result fade-in: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.

gesture_strategy: For rn-093, gestures are optional acceleration for Search-as-you-type list with debounced fetch and result fade-in; the same action must remain reachable through a labelled Pressable or accessibilityAction, with scroll-vs-pan arbitration tested on both platforms.
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-093 (Search-as-you-type list with debounced fetch and result fade-in): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Uses stable keys and virtualized row boundaries for Search-as-you-type list with debounced fetch and result fade-in; profile scroll FPS, cell re-render count, and image/text measurement on a production-sized list.
what_makes_this_native: 1) Debounced UX standard. 2) Worklet opacity. 3) FlashList recycling.

title: Combobox: input with dropdown autocomplete
design_brief: A text input that shows a dropdown list of suggestions as user types (city autocomplete, recipient search). Reference: Apple Maps search, iMessage recipient picker.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Dropdown that pushes content (use absolute overlay)
mobile_reasoning: Input + suggestion list absolutely positioned below. List filters as text changes. Tap suggestion fills input.

title: Bottom tabs with hidden tab on certain screens
design_brief: Tab bar visible on root screens but hidden on detail screens (chat detail, story viewer). Reference: WhatsApp chat detail hides tabs, Instagram story.
mobile_reasoning: Expo Router screen option tabBarStyle: {display: 'none'} on detail screens. Or animate tab bar Y with shared value tied to navigation focus.

title: Onboarding pagination dots with active state
design_brief: An onboarding screen with 3-5 pages and pagination dots at bottom that show progress. Active dot is wider/longer. Reference: Apple Watch first-launch, Headspace onboarding.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Dots fixed position when number > 5 (use scroll indicator)
mobile_reasoning: Each dot width animates between small (8) and wide (24) based on active index. Single shared value drives multiple derived widths.

title: Animated heart-rate ECG line

mobile_reasoning: Skia path with a sliding window of pulse waveform. Frame callback advances the window.

title: Photo editor filters strip
design_brief: A horizontal strip of filter thumbnails below an image (Instagram-style): tap a filter to apply. Reference: Instagram filter strip, VSCO.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Re-rendering full image on each filter tap
mobile_reasoning: Use Skia's ColorMatrix to apply filters on the fly. Each filter is a 4×5 matrix. Thumbnails show the matrix applied to a low-res preview.

title: useDeferredValue for derived heavy computations
design_brief: A list with a filter input. When typing, the list recomputes from a large dataset. Using useDeferredValue lets React keep the input responsive while the list updates async.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Synchronous heavy filter on every keystroke
Blocking the UI thread (the JS thread really)
mobile_reasoning: useDeferredValue marks the filtered query as low-priority — React renders the input change immediately and recomputes the list when idle.

stylesheet_strategy.constants: For rn-101, define named constants for useDeferredValue for derived heavy computations: MIN_TOUCH = 44, safe-area edge padding, and the component-specific sizes used by the snippet instead of anonymous inline numbers.
stylesheet_strategy.platform_differences: For rn-101, isolate cross_platform differences for useDeferredValue for derived heavy computations: shadow/elevation, keyboard/safe-area behavior, native picker/sheet/map APIs, and permission prompts stay behind a tiny adapter rather than branching through render code.
stylesheet_strategy.responsive_strategy: For rn-101, test useDeferredValue for derived heavy computations at 320pt width, large Dynamic Type, landscape/tablet split view, and notched safe areas; derive widths from useWindowDimensions or parent layout and preserve 44px touch targets.

animation_strategy: For rn-101, animate only the state transition named by useDeferredValue for derived heavy computations; keep transforms/opacity on the native/UI thread, cancel or settle on interruption, and skip motion when reduced-motion is enabled.
gesture_strategy: For rn-101, gestures are optional acceleration for useDeferredValue for derived heavy computations; the same action must remain reachable through a labelled Pressable or accessibilityAction, with scroll-vs-pan arbitration tested on both platforms.
interaction_details: Typing stays responsive even with 10000 items
List shows stale state during transitions
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-101 (useDeferredValue for derived heavy computations): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Defines a measurable hot path for useDeferredValue for derived heavy computations; compare before/after on the same device fixture and fail CI when render count, memory, or JS frame time regresses.
what_makes_this_native: 1) React 18 concurrent features in RN. 2) FlashList partnership.

title: Pull-down menu (iOS 14+) and overflow menu (Android)
design_brief: A header overflow menu. iOS: pull-down UIMenu from a button (introduced iOS 14). Android: vertical dots opening a popup. Reference: Mail iOS pull-down, any Android app overflow.

gesture_strategy: For rn-120, gestures are optional acceleration for Suspending offscreen content via Suspense + lazy; the same action must remain reachable through a labelled Pressable or accessibilityAction, with scroll-vs-pan arbitration tested on both platforms.
accessibility_considerations: Accessibility contract for rn-120 (Suspending offscreen content via Suspense + lazy): name the primary native control, keep every Pressable/TextInput/gesture target at least 44px or add hitSlop, expose an explicit non-gesture path for any swipe/pan/long-press behavior, and verify VoiceOver/TalkBack announces state changes without raw implementation details.
performance_notes: Defines a measurable hot path for Suspending offscreen content via Suspense + lazy; compare before/after on the same device fixture and fail CI when render count, memory, or JS frame time regresses.
what_makes_this_native: 1) React 18 Suspense in RN. 2) Layered with Skia skeletons. 3) Reduces initial JS payload.
